tldr: Headlines across all 28 country-buckets overwhelmingly foreground the US-Iran military exchange — waves of US airstrikes on Iran's coastal defences and Iranian drone/missile retaliation against US bases in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain — with a secondary thread noting Iran's release of a detained American as a simultaneous 'goodwill gesture'.

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only headline in the corpus to frame the conflict through Israel's deliberate non-participation — converting a bilateral US-Iran war story into an Israel-watching narrative invisible in every other outlet's title.

Only headline to foreground India's domestic protective response — shifting from the war itself to third-country labour welfare (Indian seafarers barred from Hormuz) — an angle absent from all non-India outlets.

Only headline to deploy pejorative editorial language about Trump's Hormuz toll proposal — calling it his 'latest whim and about-face' — anchoring the story in Trump's policy inconsistency rather than the military exchanges that dominate all other buckets.

bottom_line: The headline layer across 28 countries presents the US-Iran conflict as an active, escalating war — mutual strikes and a reimposed naval blockade dominate nearly every front page — while only the most editorially distant outlets (Spain, opinion magazines) step back to critique Trump's strategy, and a minor but globally-covered thread of Iran's prisoner release offers the sole diplomatic counter-signal.
